hero.question: Will the DHS shutdown end after March 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: The 10-point spike to 75.5% signals traders are pricing in Congress' historical inability to let shutdowns extend into an election year, especially with divided government finally showing signs of deal-making on spending bills. At 75% odds, the market's overweighting the "both sides will cave" narrative and sleeping on the real tail risk: a hardliner faction (House Freedom Caucus or Senate conservatives) forcing another extension past March 31st to leverage spending negotiations.

daily_take.headline: DHS Shutdown Traders Just Priced in a Deal. They're Probably Right.
daily_take.deck: The DHS shutdown resolution market jumped 10 points overnight to 75.5%, signaling real movement in backroom negotiations. The odds now reflect a Congress that actually wants to fund the department before the March 31 deadline. Smart money is already long, and if you're short this, you're betting against deal momentum that's already priced in.
